payload.summary: All five amendments are verified as real bugs through code inspection. Two amendments (fr-2-467b53d8 and fr-2-b5ae378d) describe the same cancellation-during-backoff bug from different angles — both valid. The BackendUnavailable→BinaryNotFound blanket mapping (fr-2-77935ca0) is confirmed as a semantic and retryability regression affecting auth, transport, and service-unavailability errors across multiple adapters. The process-group kill gap (fr-2-fa361db6) is confirmed: process_backend.rs only signals the direct child PID with no setsid/setpgid setup, while tmux.rs already demonstrates the correct pattern. The test assertion count mismatch (fr-2-b5e89a50) is confirmed across all four cited tests where stub failures were changed from 3 to 5 but assertions still expect 4 stage entries.

votes.rationale: Verified. engine.rs:4642-4655 passes the original `&error` (e.g. TransportFailure from the prior attempt) to `fail_run_result` when cancellation is detected during backoff sleep. The `failure_label()` function extracts `failure_class` from this error, producing "transport_failure". The test at workflow_engine_test.rs:3870 asserts `failure_class == "cancellation"` and will fail. The fix — constructing AppError::InvocationCancelled before calling fail_run_result — is correct and targeted.

Verified. error.rs:302 blanket-maps every `AppError::BackendUnavailable` to `FailureClass::BinaryNotFound`, which has `RetryRule::terminal()` (max_attempts=1, retryable=false) in retry_policy.rs:57. Multiple adapters return BackendUnavailable for non-binary conditions: missing OPENROUTER_API_KEY (process_backend.rs), 401/403 HTTP errors (openrouter_backend.rs), and GitHub HTTP/JSON failures (github.rs). These are now all mislabeled as "binary_not_found" and made permanently non-retryable, which is a semantic and operational regression. The mapping must be restricted to actual binary-not-found paths (spawn failure, exit code 127).

This describes the same cancellation-during-backoff bug as fr-2-467b53d8 from a slightly different angle and is independently valid. The tokio::select! cancellation branch at engine.rs:4642 falls through to `fail_run_result(&error, ...)` with the prior backend error. The test expects "cancellation" but gets "transport_failure". Both amendments identify the same root cause and propose the same correct fix.

Verified. process_backend.rs:365 uses `signal::kill(Pid::from_raw(pid), signal)` targeting only the direct child PID. No `setsid`, `setpgid`, or `pre_exec` calls exist to establish a process group. The cancel path (line 1304 via send_sigterm) and timeout path (line 1022 via spawn_background_reap → send_sigkill) all use the same flawed send_signal. Meanwhile tmux.rs:992-993 correctly signals both the process group (`Pid::from_raw(-pid)`) and the direct PID. Grandchildren spawned by claude/codex backends will survive SIGKILL as orphaned processes. The fix requires both process-group setup at spawn time and group-targeted signaling, following the existing tmux pattern.

Verified across all four cited locations. Each test configures `with_transient_failure(stage, 5)` but asserts `stage_entries.len() == 4`. With 5 transient failures and TransportFailure's default max_attempts=5, the stage is entered 5 times during the failed run, plus 1 more on resume = 6 total entries. The assertions expecting 4 are stale from when the stub was configured with 3 failures (3 entries in failed run + 1 on resume = 4). The fix is straightforward: update the expected counts to match the new retry budget.
